oneLiner.value: Managed cloud infrastructure for headless browsers, built for AI agents and developers who need to run browser sessions at scale without managing the underlying Chromium stack.

value.serves: Primarily developers and AI engineering teams at B2B software companies building agents, automation workflows, or data-extraction pipelines; secondary motion via Director targets non-technical operators and analysts at those same companies.
value.concept: The wedge is that every team building a web-facing AI agent independently solves the same hard infrastructure problems—fingerprinting, CAPTCHA walls, session isolation, autoscaling—and Browserbase sells that solved stack as a consumption-billed service, the same pattern Twilio used for telephony and Stripe for payments.
value.mechanism: Customers connect standard Playwright, Puppeteer, or Selenium scripts to Browserbase's cloud via a drop-in endpoint; Browserbase handles Chromium provisioning, Advanced Stealth Mode fingerprint randomization, CAPTCHA resolution, global data-center routing for latency, and SOC-2/HIPAA-compliant session isolation. Stagehand, an open-source SDK, adds a natural-language layer (act/extract/observe commands) above Playwright so agents can adapt to site changes without brittle CSS selectors. Director, launched at the Series B, generates Stagehand scripts from plain-English prompts for non-developers.
value.shortDescription: Browserbase provides managed cloud infrastructure for headless browser sessions, letting AI agents and developers spin up isolated, scalable Chromium instances via API without handling CAPTCHA solving, bot-detection evasion, proxy rotation, or session management themselves.

citations.title: Browserbase – Exa Company Profile
citations.snippet: Browserbase is the all-in-one platform developers need to host, manage, and monitor headless browsers in the cloud. Has $128.5M in total funding, most recently a Series B in June 2025. Employs 49 people (+139.1% YoY).

sourceQuality.label: Data vendor
sourceQuality.rationale: Useful for coverage, weaker for investor judgment unless confirmed elsewhere.

citations.title: Browserbase Secures $40M Series B Round, Launches No-Code Tool | Built In
citations.snippet: Browserbase raised $40M Series B led by Notable Capital, with CRV and Kleiner Perkins. Now serves over 1,000 companies and 20,000 developers. Launched Director, a no-code automation tool.

citations.title: Browserbase reels in $40M for its browser automation tools - SiliconANGLE
citations.snippet: Notable Capital led the Series B. Now valued at $300M, four times what it was worth in September. Platform can spin up thousands of browsers in a fraction of a second, compatible with Puppeteer and Selenium.

citations.title: Building the Browser Infrastructure for AI Agents with the Founder of Browserbase | Notable Capital
citations.snippet: Notable Capital led Series B. Platform used by 11x, Vercel, and Perplexity. Paul Klein IV is founder and CEO. Stagehand is the open-source automation SDK.

citations.title: Browserbase Raises $40M Series B, Launches AI Automation Tool – Upstarts Media
citations.snippet: $300M valuation, nearly 4x its Series A raised in September. 1,000+ customers, 20,000+ developer signups, 50M browser sessions run in 2025 (2x 2024's 25M). Revenue already >$3M, mostly from expanding teams. Charges on consumption basis. First salesperson hired day of Series B announcement; Klein had led all sales personally.

citations.title: Browserbase Raises $27M to Fix the Infrastructure Problem Killing Web-Browsing Agents at Scale | AgentMarketCap
citations.snippet: Independent technical analysis of Browserbase's infrastructure, competitive landscape, and funding trajectory. Series A co-led by Kleiner Perkins and CRV with Okta Ventures participation. Stagehand SDK explained: act/extract/observe natural language commands over Playwright.

citations.title: Browserbase: Web Browser Automation Company Raises $21 Million (Series A)
citations.snippet: $21M Series A co-led by CRV and Kleiner Perkins, with Okta Ventures and Reed McGinley-Stempel. Raised in less than nine months after $6.5M seed. Stagehand launched alongside Series A.

citations.title: Browserbase Raises $6.5M in Seed Funding
citations.snippet: $6.5M seed led by Kleiner Perkins with participation from Basecase Capital and AI Grant. Announced June 2024.

citations.title: Browserbase: Director Launch And $40 Million Series B Raised For Automating Web Browsing
citations.snippet: Glenn Solomon (Notable Capital managing partner) joined the board at Series B. Customers include Perplexity, Commure, 11x, Vercel, Customer.io. 50M+ browser sessions in 2025.

citations.title: Browser Use – Exa Company Profile
citations.snippet: Browser Use: open-source tool that converts website elements into text-like format for AI agents. $17.5M raised, seed led by Felicis.

sourceQuality.label: Data vendor
sourceQuality.rationale: Useful for coverage, weaker for investor judgment unless confirmed elsewhere.

citations.title: browserless – Exa Company Profile
citations.snippet: Browserless provides hosted browser pool for Puppeteer/Playwright/API connections. 11 employees. Single undisclosed funding round in 2021.

sourceQuality.label: Data vendor
sourceQuality.rationale: Useful for coverage, weaker for investor judgment unless confirmed elsewhere.
